# Stepper

Organizes a multi-step task and exposes the user's current position.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Controlled step](#controlled-step)
- [Linear, completed, and disabled steps](#linear-completed-and-disabled-steps)
- [Content and actions](#content-and-actions)
- [Orientation and keyboard behavior](#orientation-and-keyboard-behavior)
- [Choosing a sequence component](#choosing-a-sequence-component)

## Installation

Add the Stepper components from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/stepper
```

## Usage

Use Stepper for a task with a small, ordered sequence whose steps are meaningful to the user. Give every `StepperItem` a unique numeric `step`, then compose its indicator, title, and optional description inside `StepperTrigger`. Render a separator only between adjacent items.

**Stepper example**

```vue
<template>
  <Stepper :default-value="1" class="w-full max-w-xl items-start">
    <StepperItem v-for="step in steps" :key="step.step" :step="step.step" class="flex-1">
      <StepperTrigger>
        <StepperIndicator>{{ step.step }}</StepperIndicator>
        <StepperTitle>{{ step.title }}</StepperTitle>
        <StepperDescription>{{ step.description }}</StepperDescription>
      </StepperTrigger>
      <StepperSeparator v-if="step.step !== steps.length" class="h-px flex-1" />
    </StepperItem>
  </Stepper>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Stepper,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperIndicator,
  StepperItem,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperTrigger,
} from '@/Components/Stepper'

const steps = [
  { step: 1, title: 'Account', description: 'Your details' },
  { step: 2, title: 'Workspace', description: 'Team settings' },
  { step: 3, title: 'Finish', description: 'Review changes' },
]
</script>
```

## Controlled step

Bind `v-model` to a number when the displayed step must stay aligned with form validation, navigation, or saved draft state. Use `default-value` only when the Stepper may own its initial state. The root slot also exposes navigation helpers and state, but application rules should remain the source of truth for whether a step can be entered.

```vue
<Stepper v-model="currentStep" linear>
  <!-- StepperItem components -->
</Stepper>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from 'vue'

const currentStep = ref(1)
</script>
```

## Linear, completed, and disabled steps

The local Stepper is linear by default, preventing users from jumping more than one step ahead. Set `:linear="false"` only when steps may be visited freely. Use a step item's `completed` prop only when completion is known from real application state, and `disabled` when a step cannot currently be selected. Do not mark every earlier step complete merely because the user navigated forward if its data is still invalid.

```vue
<StepperItem
  :step="2"
  :completed="billingIsValid"
  :disabled="!accountIsValid"
>
  <!-- Trigger, indicator, title, and description -->
</StepperItem>
```

## Content and actions

Stepper describes and changes the current position; it does not render each step's form content or provide Next and Back actions. Keep the active panel close to the step list, label the navigation Buttons by outcome, validate before advancing, and preserve entered values when moving backward.

## Orientation and keyboard behavior

The root forwards `orientation` to Reka so arrow-key behavior matches a horizontal or vertical sequence. The local styling is horizontal by default; a vertical Stepper also needs explicit layout classes for the root, items, and separators. Do not set a vertical orientation while leaving the visual arrangement horizontal.

Step titles should remain visible and concise. Indicators alone are not enough: numbers and icons need the associated title and description to explain the step. Disabled steps must not be the only place users can learn why progress is blocked.

The root exposes the group with the accessible label “progress” and includes a polite live status announcing “Step n of total” when the model changes. Avoid adding another live announcement for the same step transition, and ensure validation errors are announced independently beside the active content.

## Choosing a sequence component

> **Use Progress for non-interactive completion: [Progress](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/progress)**
>
> When users only need to see how much background work remains, use Progress rather than presenting completion as a set of selectable steps.

> **Use Tabs for peer views: [Tabs](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/tabs)**
>
> Use Tabs when sections are peers and may be visited in any order. A Stepper implies sequence, progress, and a current stage in one task.

Hosted documentation: [Stepper](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/stepper)
